Alchemy Mechanics: {"mechanics": "To make potions you need a Mortar and Pestle (minimum) plus 2-4 ingredients sharing at least one effect. Optional apparatus: Retort (boosts positive effects), Calcinator (increases all effect magnitude/duration), Alembic (reduces negative effects). Success chance = Alchemy skill + Intelligence/10 + Luck/10. Potion value = (Alchemy + Intelligence/10 + Luck/10) * Mortar quality. Effect visibility: 0-14 skill sees no effects, 15-29 sees 1st, 30-44 sees 2, 45-59 sees 3, 60+ sees all 4.", "useful_potions": [{"name": "Restore Health", "ingredients": ["Marshmerrow", "Wickwheat"], "effect": "Restore Health. Both are cheap and common, making this the most accessible healing potion."}, {"name": "Restore Fatigue", "ingredients": ["Saltrice", "Large Kwama Egg"], "effect": "Restore Fatigue. Abundant ingredients found in most towns and farms."}, {"name": "Fortify Intelligence", "ingredients": ["Ash Yam", "Bloat"], "effect": "Fortify Intelligence. Boosts alchemy skill indirectly, enabling the Intelligence-stacking exploit for extremely powerful potions."}, {"name": "Restore Magicka", "ingredients": ["Comberry", "Frost Salts"], "effect": "Restore Magicka. Valuable potion; Frost Salts found on Frost Atronachs."}, {"name": "Cure Common Disease", "ingredients": ["Green Lichen", "Chokeweed"], "effect": "Cure Common Disease. Very cheap ingredients found everywhere in the wilderness."}, {"name": "Levitate", "ingredients": ["Racer Plumes", "Trama Root"], "effect": "Levitate. Essential for exploration; Trama Root is abundant in Ashlands."}, {"name": "Paralyze", "ingredients": ["Wickwheat", "Large Kwama Egg"], "effect": "Paralyze. Extremely powerful in combat; cheap ingredients."}, {"name": "Fortify Strength", "ingredients": ["Ash Yam", "Dreugh Wax"], "effect": "Fortify Strength. High-value potion, Dreugh Wax is worth 100g."}, {"name": "Restore Health (expensive)", "ingredients": ["Corkbulb Root", "Resin"], "effect": "Restore Health. Slightly more expensive ingredients but still common."}, {"name": "Invisibility", "ingredients": ["Bittergreen Petals", "Diamond"], "effect": "Invisibility. Very high value potion due to Diamond ingredient."}, {"name": "Water Walking", "ingredients": ["Kwama Cuttle", "Ampoule Pod"], "effect": "Water Walking. Useful for crossing water; ingredients are lightweight and common."}, {"name": "Resist Magicka", "ingredients": ["Ash Salts", "Ash Salts"], "effect": "Resist Magicka. Ash Salts has this as both 2nd and 4th effect; pair with any other Resist Magicka source."}, {"name": "Fortify Health (profitable)", "ingredients": ["Shalk Resin", "Large Kwama Egg"], "effect": "Fortify Health. Shalk Resin (50g) makes this a valuable potion to sell."}]}
Enchanting Mechanics: {"mechanics": "Enchanting applies permanent magical effects to items using trapped souls. Two methods: (1) Pay an NPC enchanter (guaranteed success, expensive), or (2) Self-enchant (free but risky). Self-enchant success formula: (0.75 + Fatigue%/2) * (1 - 0.5*isConstant) * (Enchant + Intelligence/5 + Luck/10 - 3*EnchantmentPoints). Key insight: enchantment points cost matters 3x as much as Enchant skill, which matters 5x as much as Intelligence, which matters 2x as much as Luck. Constant Effect enchantments require souls with 400+ capacity (Golden Saints, Ascended Sleepers). Best enchanting items: Ebony Staff (90 enchant points for weapons), Daedric Tower Shield (225 points for armor).", "soul_gems": [{"type": "Petty", "capacity": 30}, {"type": "Lesser", "capacity": 60}, {"type": "Common", "capacity": 120}, {"type": "Greater", "capacity": 180}, {"type": "Grand", "capacity": 600}, {"type": "Azura's Star", "capacity": 15000}], "notable_items": [{"name": "Ebony Staff", "enchant_capacity": 90, "note": "Highest weapon enchantment capacity - over 4x the next best staff"}, {"name": "Daedric Tower Shield", "enchant_capacity": 225, "note": "Highest armor enchantment capacity, ideal for constant effect enchantments"}, {"name": "Azura's Star", "enchant_capacity": null, "note": "Reusable soul gem with 15000 capacity; not destroyed after use. Obtained from Azura's quest."}], "constant_effect_sources": ["Golden Saint (soul value 400)", "Ascended Sleeper (soul value 400+)", "Other large/rare creatures with 400+ soul value"]}
Combat: {"mechanics": {"hit_chance_formula": "Hit Rate = Weapon Skill + (Agility / 5) + (Luck / 10), multiplied by fatigue factor (0.75 to 1.25). Fortify Attack effects also modify accuracy. Final hit chance = Attacker Hit Rate - Defender Evasion.", "damage_formula": "Damage = (Weapon Base Damage * Strength Modifier * Condition Modifier * Critical Hit Multiplier) / Armor Reduction. Strength Modifier = (Strength + 50) / 100, so at 50 Strength you deal normal damage.", "armor_formula": "Damage / min(1 + Armor Rating / Damage, 4). Diminishing returns; max 75% damage reduction.", "blocking": "Block chance = Defender Block Rate - Attacker Hit Chance. Ranges 10%-50% with shield equipped. Standing still increases block effectiveness by 25%.", "fatigue_effects": "Depleted fatigue reduces hit accuracy and damage output by up to 25%. Always keep fatigue high in combat."}, "weapon_types": [{"type": "Axes", "best_attack": "Chop", "advantages": "Highest damage output", "disadvantages": "Heavy, slow, narrow range"}, {"type": "Blunt Weapons", "best_attack": "Varies", "advantages": "Predictable damage ranges; staves have high enchantment capacity", "disadvantages": "Lower damage than axes"}, {"type": "Long Blades", "best_attack": "Versatile", "advantages": "Good for all attack types; most abundant weapon type", "disadvantages": "Limited range"}, {"type": "Short Blades", "best_attack": "Thrust/Chop", "advantages": "Fastest weapons available", "disadvantages": "Lower damage per hit"}, {"type": "Spears", "best_attack": "Thrust", "advantages": "Longest reach for distance combat", "disadvantages": "Weak chops/slashes; fewer quality options"}, {"type": "Hand-to-Hand", "best_attack": "Any", "advantages": "Drains fatigue first causing knockdowns; effective 1v1", "disadvantages": "No weapon damage bonus; requires close range"}], "tips": ["Sujamma raises Strength by 50 points for 1 minute; multiple bottles stack for devastating damage.", "Maintain full fatigue before engaging enemies - it affects both hit chance and damage by up to 25%.", "Match your attack type (chop/slash/thrust) to your weapon's strongest damage range.", "Keep weapons repaired - a weapon at 50% condition deals only 50% normal damage.", "Cast Levitate on melee-only enemies to severely hamper their movement.", "Hand-to-hand is most effective against single opponents; stagger effects prevent counterattacks.", "Standing still while blocking increases effectiveness by 25%.", "Isolate enemies using terrain; exploit weapon reach and attack speed differences.", "For ranged combat, avoid heavy armor and maintain distance."]}
Character Creation: {"recommended_races": [{"race": "Redguard (Male)", "why": "High starting Strength and Endurance. Excellent for warrior builds. Natural Adrenaline Rush power."}, {"race": "Nord", "why": "Excels in strength-based builds. High starting Strength and Endurance. Frost resistance useful in Solstheim."}, {"race": "High Elf (Altmer)", "why": "Gains significantly more Magicka than other races (+100 Magicka). Best for pure mage builds despite weakness to magic."}, {"race": "Dark Elf (Dunmer)", "why": "Well-rounded with fire resistance. Good evasion abilities. Versatile for hybrid builds. Fits lore naturally."}, {"race": "Orc", "why": "Unique bonuses to Armorer and Block skills. High Endurance. Berserk power is devastating in early game combat."}, {"race": "Breton", "why": "50% Magicka resistance and bonus Magicka. Excellent for spellsword or battlemage builds. Dragon Skin power absorbs spells."}, {"race": "Argonian", "why": "Free Water Breathing ability. 75% disease resistance. Good for exploring underwater areas without potions."}, {"race": "Khajiit", "why": "Free Night Eye ability. Good Agility for stealth builds. Eye of Night power aids dungeon exploration."}], "recommended_birthsigns": [{"sign": "The Lady", "why": "+25 Endurance and +25 Personality. Endurance boost increases HP per level permanently. Great for any build."}, {"sign": "The Atronach", "why": "+2.0x INT Magicka multiplier but no natural Magicka regeneration. Massive Magicka pool. Absorb 50% of spells. Requires shrine visits or Ancestral Ghost trick to restore Magicka."}, {"sign": "The Lover", "why": "+25 Agility. Paralyze touch power once per day. Agility boosts hit chance and evasion."}, {"sign": "The Steed", "why": "+25 Speed. Faster movement from the start. Quality of life improvement for the entire game."}, {"sign": "The Warrior", "why": "+10 Attack. Simple and effective for combat-focused characters, especially early game."}], "custom_class_tips": ["If this is your first time playing, use a predefined class or the class quiz rather than custom creation.", "Plan race, class, and birthsign together as they interconnect - all three shape your attributes, abilities, and spells.", "Spread Major/Minor skills across different governing attributes to maximize level-up multipliers.", "Include at least one combat skill as Major to survive early encounters.", "Consider putting skills you use constantly as Misc skills to control leveling pace.", "Prioritize Endurance as a favored attribute - it increases HP every level and the bonus is retroactive only if gained early."], "tips": ["Choose race based on intended playstyle rather than seeking a single 'best' option.", "High Elves get the most Magicka but have elemental weaknesses - plan mitigation.", "The Atronach birthsign requires strategic planning: use shrine blessings, summon Ancestral Ghost and punch it to trigger spell absorption, or fight Bull Netches for Magicka recovery.", "Argonians and several other races have 75% disease resistance naturally.", "Dark Elves fit the game's lore most naturally and are solid all-rounders."]}
Leveling: {"mechanics": "You gain a level after 10 increases to Major or Minor skills (training, skill books, and natural use all count). You must rest (not wait) to level up. At level-up, choose 3 attributes to increase. Miscellaneous skill increases do NOT count toward the 10-skill threshold but DO affect attribute multipliers.", "attribute_multipliers": {"description": "The multiplier for each attribute depends on how many skill increases (Major, Minor, AND Miscellaneous) in skills governed by that attribute since last level-up.", "table": [{"skill_increases": "0", "multiplier": "1x"}, {"skill_increases": "1-4", "multiplier": "2x"}, {"skill_increases": "5-7", "multiplier": "3x"}, {"skill_increases": "8-9", "multiplier": "4x"}, {"skill_increases": "10+", "multiplier": "5x"}]}, "optimization": "Plan skill increases so you get exactly 10 increases in each of 3 different governing attributes, with exactly 10 of those being Major/Minor skills. This gives you three 5x multipliers per level. Spread your Major/Minor skills across different attributes at character creation to make this easier.", "tips": ["Prioritize Endurance early - it increases Health gained at every level up.", "Track skill progress manually; the UI does not show counts toward attribute multipliers.", "Both trainer-purchased and skill book increases count toward leveling.", "Miscellaneous skills count toward attribute multipliers but not the 10-skill level threshold - use this strategically.", "Do not let Major/Minor progress exceed 10 before resting - excess increases do NOT roll over to next level.", "Avoid unplanned skill increases that waste multiplier potential.", "Coordinate equipment choices and combat tactics with your leveling plan.", "Stay adaptable when accidental skill increases occur - adjust your plan rather than forcing it."]}
Money Making: [{"method": "Potion Making (Alchemy)", "gold_estimate": "500-2000 per batch", "requirements": "Buy ingredients from restocking merchants. Higher quality alchemy apparatus yields better potions worth more gold.", "difficulty": "Easy"}, {"method": "Glass Dagger Repair", "gold_estimate": "500-1500 per dagger", "requirements": "Buy damaged glass daggers from Lucretinaus Olcinius in Vivec St. Delyn Plaza and other merchants. Repair with Armorer skill and sell.", "difficulty": "Easy"}, {"method": "Daedric Weapon from Vassir-Didanat Cave", "gold_estimate": "1000-2000", "requirements": "Travel to Vassir-Didanat Cave, find the ebony mine, report to Dram Bero in Vivec for a Daedric weapon reward.", "difficulty": "Easy"}, {"method": "Glass Netch Dagger (Gnaar Mok)", "gold_estimate": "800-1200", "requirements": "Travel to Gnaar Mok and locate a hollow tree stump containing the dagger.", "difficulty": "Easy"}, {"method": "Sword of White Woe", "gold_estimate": "1500-2500", "requirements": "Located in Balmora guard tower. Hide behind a pillar to avoid guard detection while taking it.", "difficulty": "Medium"}, {"method": "Soul Hunting", "gold_estimate": "1000-5000 per cycle", "requirements": "Soul gems (buy from restocking merchants) and Soultrap spell. Trap creature souls and sell filled gems.", "difficulty": "Medium"}, {"method": "Glass Armor Set (steal)", "gold_estimate": "2000-3000", "requirements": "Lockpicking or Open spell at 80 points. Stealth skills recommended.", "difficulty": "Medium"}, {"method": "Orcish Armor (Ghorak Manor)", "gold_estimate": "1500-2500", "requirements": "Access the top floor of Ghorak Manor in Caldera.", "difficulty": "Medium"}, {"method": "Looting Daedra (renewable)", "gold_estimate": "500-1500 per kill", "requirements": "Access to respawning Daedra locations. Combat capability to defeat Daedra.", "difficulty": "Medium"}, {"method": "Looting Dark Brotherhood Assassins", "gold_estimate": "1000-3000 per assassin", "requirements": "Tribunal DLC installed. Assassins attack periodically while resting.", "difficulty": "Medium-Hard"}, {"method": "Master Alchemy Set (steal)", "gold_estimate": "800-1500", "requirements": "Steal from Mages Guild or Nalcarya's shop. Stealth or high security skill needed.", "difficulty": "Medium-Hard"}, {"method": "Looting Guards", "gold_estimate": "800-2000 per guard", "requirements": "Commit a minor crime, resist arrest, and defeat the guards for their equipment.", "difficulty": "Hard"}]

Some races have 75% natural resistance (Argonians, Bosmer, Altmer, Redguards)."}, {"method": "100% Disease Resistance", "details": "Achievable through main quest progression (Corprus cure grants immunity)."}]}
General Tips: ["Dropped items never disappear - establish loot caches near transport hubs (silt striders, boats, Mages Guild teleporters).", "Use Mark/Recall spell combo near a permanent container for unlimited portable storage.", "Teleportation spells (Mark/Recall, Divine/Almsivi Intervention) work even when overencumbered - use for loot transport.", "Cast 100% Resist Magicka on yourself, then immediately equip Boots of Blinding Speed to gain speed without blindness.", "Create cheap 1-point magnitude training spells to efficiently level magic skills.", "Lock spell + lockpick combo lets you train Security skill anywhere with containers.", "Jump constantly on hills and staircases to train Acrobatics passively.", "Let mudcrabs attack you without retaliating to train armor skills safely.", "Swim into walls repeatedly to train Athletics.", "Cast Fortify Personality before talking to trainers to lower their prices.", "Items with unfamiliar names may be quest-related - store them separately, never sell them.", "Different merchants offer varying prices - test multiple vendors for the best deals.", "Creature merchants (Creeper in Caldera, Mudcrab merchant) buy at base price with no disposition requirement.", "Crates and barrels respawn random loot when you leave and re-enter a cell without taking anything first.", "Remove armor or go invisible to talk to normally-hostile NPCs who refuse dialogue.", "Save often and in multiple slots - Morrowind can be unforgiving.", "Fortify Skill spells can raise any trainer to master level for cheaper training.", "Enchant items with constant effect Restore Health or Fortify Attribute for permanent passive bonuses.", "For Atronach birthsign: summon Ancestral Ghost (6 Magicka cost) and punch it to trigger spell absorption for Magicka recovery.", "For Atronach birthsign: fight Bull Netches - their Poisonbloom spell can restore up to 39 Magicka per cast via absorption."]
